title: Indian Quiche
description: A unique twist on the classic quiche, blending Indian spices with the French dish. This quiche is perfect for brunch or dinner.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the wholemeal flour and all-purpose flour.
Add the softened butter and use a pastry blender or your fingertips to work it into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add the milk, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together in a ball.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead a few times until it becomes smooth.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch (3 mm).
Transfer the dough to a 9-inch (23 cm) tart pan with a removable bottom.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs and a pinch of salt.
Arrange the eggs in the pastry shell, leaving a 1-inch (2.5 cm) border around the edges.
Bake the quiche for 35-40 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the center is set.
Remove the quiche from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
